title: Hiker rescued by helicopter after 50-foot fall in Huntington Ravine
author: John Waller
bullets: A 24-year-old Quebec City man was rescued by helicopter after a 50-foot fall on Mount Washington's Huntington Ravine Trail.
The New Hampshire National Guard facilitated the rescue due to the terrain and the hiker's serious injuries.
Officials warn the Huntington Ravine Trail is not suitable for casual hikers and requires climbing gear and proper footwear.

title: State trooper relieved of duty after allegedly being captured on video knocking out other trooper in bar fight
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: A Massachusetts State Police trooper was relieved of duty after a bar fight in Quincy with another trooper.
Surveillance footage shows the trooper allegedly knocking another trooper unconscious at ReelHouse Marina Bay.
The incident is under investigation by the State Police's Office of Professional Integrity and Accountability.

title: What makes a ship a ‘tall ship’?
author: Madeline Bilis
bullets: The term 'tall ship' originates from a 1902 poem, not from the ship's height.
Tall ships are large, traditionally rigged sailing vessels used for sail training, not defined by specific dimensions.
Sail Boston 2026 will feature various tall ships, highlighting their cultural significance and historical connection.

title: NBA insider offers the latest on the Jaylen Brown trade rumors
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Celtics are actively seeking trade options for Jaylen Brown, including multi-team deals, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
Boston's asking price for Jaylen Brown includes up to four first-round picks, creating uncertainty among rival NBA teams.
Celtics' trade discussions revolve around challenges in determining Jaylen Brown's market value given Boston's high demands.

title: Sewer spill closes North Shore beaches after Haverhill pipe breaks
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Two sewer main breaks in Haverhill released wastewater into Merrimack River, causing beach closures on the North Shore.
Newburyport's Plum Island Beach and Salisbury beaches warned swimmers due to potential sewer bacteria exposure.
Haverhill officials investigating pipe failures; beach access restricted until further water quality assessments.

title: What Boston meteorologists are saying about ‘dangerous heat and humidity’ this Fourth of July week
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Excessive heat watch issued for Wednesday to Friday in Boston with heat index reaching 105-110 degrees.
National Weather Service warns of heat indices above 100F and high humidity, minimal overnight relief.
Meteorologist Chris Lambert forecasts temperatures nearing 100 degrees by Thursday, posing health risks.

title: Kayla Burton announces engagement to Patriots coach Jeremy Springer
author: Gary Dzen
bullets: Kayla Burton and Patriots coach Jeremy Springer announce engagement on social media with photos by Alyse Arauz Photography.
Burton is a broadcaster for NBC Sports Boston and ESPN, and the daughter of newscaster Steve Burton.
Jeremy Springer joined the Patriots in 2024 as special teams coordinator after playing college football at UTEP.

title: 31-year-old state trooper killed in off-duty motorcycle crash
author: John Waller
bullets: Trooper Jacob Mick, 31, was killed in a motorcycle crash with a passenger vehicle on Route 122 in Grafton.
The crash occurred around 6 p.m. Sunday, leaving Mick dead at the scene, according to the Worcester County DA.
The passenger vehicle's driver was hospitalized with non-life-threatening injuries; the incident remains under investigation.

title: Brookline’s regal Webber-Bouve Mansion hits market for $4.3m
author: Regina Cole
bullets: The Webber-Bouve Mansion in Brookline is listed for sale at $4.3 million.
Built in 1885, the mansion features a Queen Anne style and retains original Victorian architectural elements.
The 6,227-square-foot home includes seven bedrooms, three full bathrooms, and extensive stained glass details.

title: Motor coach bus catches on fire on I-95 in N.H.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A motor coach bus caught fire on Interstate 95 in Hampton, N.H., on Sunday afternoon, according to fire officials.
All passengers safely evacuated, with no injuries or hospitalizations reported, thanks to the bus driver's quick actions.
The fire, possibly originating in the wheel area, caused traffic delays; the cause is under investigation.
